incidents.victim: Ukrainian prosecutors and Balkan/NATO government agencies
incidents.vector: Spearphishing and credential theft against prosecutorial email accounts, with the stolen inbox data inadvertently left exposed on an attacker-controlled server discovered by researchers.
incidents.impact: At least 284 email accounts were compromised between September 2024 and March 2026, including 170+ Ukrainian prosecutors/investigators probing corruption and Russian collaborators, 67 Romanian Air Force accounts, 27 Greek defense accounts, plus Bulgarian and Serbian officials. Thousands of stolen emails were recovered.

incidents.vector: Attempted intrusion into an operational technology (OT) control system at a Swedish thermal power plant in spring 2025; Swedish security services attributed the operation to actors tied to Russian intelligence.
incidents.impact: Intrusion was unsuccessful due to built-in security protections. Sweden disclosed the incident publicly on April 15, 2026 and said similar attempts have been observed in Norway, Denmark and on a larger scale in Poland — marking a shift from DDoS to destructive OT-focused attacks on European energy infrastructure.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2026-32201, an improper input validation flaw in Microsoft SharePoint Server enabling unauthenticated network-based spoofing of trusted content.
incidents.impact: Actively exploited in the wild before patching; Microsoft addressed it in its April Patch Tuesday (second-largest ever, 165 CVEs). CISA added it to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. Enables phishing, unauthorized data manipulation, and social-engineering pivots within enterprise SharePoint environments.

incidents.vector: Publicly released proof-of-concept exploit for 'BlueHammer' (CVE-2026-33825) — a time-of-check-to-time-of-use race condition and path confusion in Windows Defender's signature update system enabling local privilege escalation via SAM access and pass-the-hash.
incidents.impact: Disgruntled researcher leaked working exploit code on April 2, before Microsoft patched it on April 14. Exploit enables full system takeover by local users; experts warned ransomware and APT groups typically weaponize such bugs within days.

incidents.vector: Human error during a planned system update caused protected address fields to be visible to recipients of domestic payment transactions.
incidents.impact: Approximately 20,600 client home addresses were inadvertently exposed to outside recipients over an extended period before discovery. No financial data was exposed. Disclosed publicly on April 14, 2026.

incidents.vector: Two former employees filmed customer data displayed in Kraken's internal systems; a criminal group then attempted to extort Kraken using those videos as leverage.
incidents.impact: Kraken refused to pay, fired the employees, and notified affected users and law enforcement. Exposure was limited to screen recordings rather than bulk data exfiltration.

incidents.vector: Data was stolen from Hallmark's Salesforce tenant during the large multi-victim Salesforce campaign of 2025; when Hallmark refused to pay a ransom, the group (ShinyHunters) published the stolen database.
incidents.impact: Personal data of approximately 1.7 million Hallmark customers was leaked publicly and indexed by Have I Been Pwned. The same criminal group also reportedly used stolen credentials to exfiltrate 90 GB of data from the European Commission's AWS environment this month.

incidents.vector: Unauthorized access to NYK's marine fuel procurement (bunker) system in late March 2026; entry vector not disclosed.
incidents.impact: NYK took the bunker procurement platform offline and disclosed that employee data was accessed and likely stolen. NYK is part of Ocean Network Express, the world's sixth-largest container shipping group — raising shipping-sector supply-chain concerns.

incidents.vector: Ransomware intrusion against Die Linke's IT network in late March 2026 attributed to the Qilin ransomware operation.
incidents.impact: Party shut down affected systems. Qilin stole employee records, though the party said no member data was compromised. Fits a broader 2025-2026 pattern of Qilin targeting European political and manufacturing organizations.

incidents.victim: TP-Link and MikroTik SOHO router owners (global, 23 US states)
incidents.vector: GRU actors exploited known vulnerabilities to steal credentials from 18,000+ end-of-life TP-Link and MikroTik routers, modified DNS settings to point to attacker-controlled resolvers, and used that position to conduct adversary-in-the-middle attacks against Microsoft Outlook Web Access to harvest OAuth authentication tokens, passwords and emails.
incidents.impact: Campaign active since at least 2024; at peak in December 2025 ensnared 18,000+ routers across more than 200 targeted organizations including foreign-affairs ministries, law enforcement, and third-party email providers. On April 7, 2026 DOJ and FBI executed 'Operation Masquerade,' a court-authorized technical takedown that reset DNS settings on compromised US routers across 23 states.

incidents.vector: A self-propagating worm ('CanisterWorm') was deployed through the same compromised infrastructure used in the Trivy supply chain attack; it targets exposed Docker APIs, Kubernetes clusters, Redis servers and the React2Shell vulnerability, and executes data-wiping payloads on systems whose locale is set to Farsi or whose timezone matches Iran.
incidents.impact: Destructive wiper campaign launched against Iranian cloud and enterprise systems, injecting politically motivated attacks into the criminal group's otherwise financial operations. Tied to a broader ongoing supply-chain intrusion wave by the same actor.

incidents.vector: Unauthenticated remote code execution via CVE-2026-35616 (CVSS 9.8) in Fortinet's FortiClient Enterprise Management Server, actively exploited as a zero-day since at least March 31, 2026.
incidents.impact: Fortinet issued an emergency hotfix over Easter weekend; a full patch remains pending. Shadowserver identified nearly 2,000 publicly exposed FortiClient EMS instances. CISA added the flaw to the KEV catalog. Exploitation ramped up rapidly after the hotfix disclosure.

incidents.vector: Operators impersonated a quantitative trading firm at a major crypto conference in December 2025 and built trust over months (including depositing $1M into a Drift Ecosystem Vault). They compromised Drift employee devices via malicious Apple TestFlight app and VS Code extensions and used that foothold to obtain multi-signature approvals from Drift's Security Council before draining the treasury on April 1.
incidents.impact: Approximately $280-285 million in crypto assets stolen from Drift. Attributed to North Korea's 'Contagious Interview' cluster. Linked to a broader wave of 2025-2026 DPRK crypto thefts including the $1.5B Bybit heist.

incidents.vector: Social-engineering attack against Hims & Hers' customer support system in February 2026 exposed information contained in support tickets.
incidents.impact: Customer names and email addresses stolen, along with any personal or medical data shared in support interactions. Disclosed publicly in early April 2026.

incidents.vector: Successful phishing attack against Jones Day employees.
incidents.impact: Hackers accessed and posted client materials online from one of the largest US corporate law firms (2,400+ lawyers). Jones Day is notable for representing the Trump 2016/2020 campaigns; the stolen client files may carry political and corporate sensitivity.

incidents.vector: Undisclosed intrusion into Hasbro's IT systems in late March/early April 2026.
incidents.impact: Hasbro shut down affected systems, deployed intermediary infrastructure to continue orders and shipping, and disclosed in an SEC 8-K that full recovery would take weeks. No ransomware or extortion group had publicly claimed responsibility as of mid-April.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack on the shared regional emergency communications center serving four Massachusetts towns.
incidents.impact: Disrupted police and fire department dispatch services and phone lines across the towns of Ashby, Dunstable, Pepperell and Townsend for several days in early April 2026 — a tangible life-safety risk during the outage.

incidents.victim: Software supply chain users (Trivy, Checkmarx KICS, LiteLLM, VS Code plug-ins)
incidents.vector: Using stolen publishing tokens (likely from infostealer logs), the self-styled 'TeamPCP' group injected credential-harvesting malware into 76 of 77 versions of Aqua Security's Trivy GitHub Action, Checkmarx's KICS GitHub Action, two Checkmarx VS Code plug-ins on OpenVSX, and LiteLLM (Python package, ~97M monthly downloads). Payload harvested SSH keys, cloud credentials, API tokens, Docker configs and crypto wallet data, exfiltrating to lookalike domains.
incidents.impact: Cascading downstream compromises across five ecosystems in roughly five days; blast radius is enormous because LiteLLM alone is present in ~36% of cloud environments per Wiz. Downstream victims publicly confirmed include Cisco (source code stolen from dev environment), the European Commission (90 GB exfiltrated from AWS via these stolen credentials), Mercor (AI hiring platform valued at $10B, prompting Meta to pause its contract) and BuddyBoss (~250 compromised university sites). The group reportedly partnered with the Lapsus$ extortion crew.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack against Utah-based NewspaperArchive in late February 2026; technical details not publicly disclosed.
incidents.impact: Outage of the NewspaperArchive service is still ongoing more than six weeks later, disrupting access to digital newspaper archives at libraries across the United States. Many libraries that relied on the service for local archives have lost user-facing access.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack in February 2026 against the IT systems of Italy's top art museum, with reported extortion demands.
incidents.impact: Uffizi moved its most valuable works — including House of Medici holdings with paintings by Leonardo, Raphael and Botticelli — into secured bank vaults as a precaution. The museum said no passwords or internal security maps were stolen and the situation is 'nothing like the Louvre' incident.
